# Yazio: Contador de calorias · Fitness

Generated: Jul 22, 2026, 11:11 AM

## **Contents** {#toc}

- [Bugs and stability](#theme-bugs_stability)
- [Ads pressure](#theme-ads)


## **Bugs and stability** {#theme-bugs_stability}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      5 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 26, 2026 - Jul 11, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Cam_Cam._, Jun 29, 2026, 2/5: "I've been using it for 2 years with 600flammes but it's been bugging for 2 weeks so I uninstalled it but now it doesn't want to reopen, it leaves the app on its own and I'm going to lose my flames this evening!"
            2. _nyc driver 1234_, Jul 11, 2026, 4/5: "I use this app to lose weight after an operation. It’s helpful ! Some improvement and bug fix: - connected apps: active calories from iOS health cannot be deleted and disconnecting doesn’t work consistently, garmin connect imports total calories instead of active calories of sport - when item not found: use AI to take picture or nutrition label instead of manual import, manual imports are annoying - data shown: filter by nutritional information like calories and protein to know which foods to eat / avoid. Even some labeling / color score or “diet list” Could be useful"
            3. __MarkCard__, Jul 8, 2026, 4/5: "Excellent app, intuitive and reliable. It does what it promises and the micro-bugs found are negligible. However, I would like to see more in-depth KPIs and statistics, the possibility of viewing the time of entering meals and activities and, above all, greater customization of the food diary. It would be very useful to be able to create or rename meals (e.g. breakfast, morning snack, lunch, afternoon snack, dinner and evening snack), instead of being limited to a fixed structure. With these improvements it would easily become a 5 star app."

## **Ads pressure** {#theme-ads}

   **Ads pressure.**
      1 recent review(s) mention ads pressure.
      Review window: Jul 1, 2026 - Jul 1, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are noticing ad pressure. This can signal monetization friction and an opening for a cleaner competitor.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _langustarews_, Jul 1, 2026, 1/5: "Im reviewing the free version only, usually I like to check if I like the app enough to pay for the pro version. Well, I didn’t get to that point because the amount of ads they throw at you at every step is making it impossible to try."
